title: Errors 6
description: Using `Box<dyn Error>` as the error type allows any error to be returned, which is useful for quick prototyping.
instructions: Use Box<dyn Error> to handle multiple error types in a single function.
hints: A String can be converted to Box<dyn Error> using `.into()`.
Alternatively, use `Err(err.into())` to box the string error.
Box<dyn Error> accepts any type that implements the Error trait.
explanation: `Box<dyn Error>` is a trait object that can hold any error type. It is convenient for functions that might produce different error types, especially during prototyping. The `.into()` method converts concrete error types into the boxed trait object. For production code, custom error enums or crates like `thiserror` and `anyhow` are often preferred.
